description: Implement the Human-in-the-Room Directives domain layer: new event type, types, state projector handler, Zod schema, context assembler integration, prompt builder formatting, API route, and property-based tests
steps: Add 'human-directive' to the EventType union in src/types/domain.ts (making it 14 event types total)
Add HumanDirective interface to src/types/domain.ts with fields: id (string), text (string), createdAt (string), source ('human'), active (boolean)
Add humanDirectives: HumanDirective[] to the SessionState interface in src/types/domain.ts
Add humanDirectives field to WorkspaceContext interface in src/types/domain.ts
Add handleHumanDirective handler function in src/lib/state-projector.ts that appends a HumanDirective to state.humanDirectives from event content
Add case 'human-directive' to the switch statements in projectSessionState and applyEvents in src/lib/state-projector.ts
Initialize humanDirectives: [] in createEmptySessionState() in src/lib/state-projector.ts
Create src/schemas/human-directive.ts with a Zod schema for validating directive input (text: 1-500 chars, non-empty after trim)
Update src/lib/context-assembler.ts to include humanDirectives from projected state in the assembled WorkspaceContext (at same priority level as constraints - Priority 3)

Update src/lib/prompt-builder.ts to add a formatHumanDirectives function and include human directives in the stable system block after constraints section, clearly delimited as '## Human Team Directives (from human engineer)'
Create src/app/api/sessions/[sessionId]/directives/route.ts with POST endpoint that validates input, creates a human-directive event via eventStore.appendEvent, and returns the created directive

Add property-based tests to src/lib/state-projector.test.ts proving: (1) replay reconstructs directives correctly, (2) directive ordering is deterministic, (3) snapshots and full replay remain equivalent for directives, (4) unrelated events do not mutate directive state
Add a focused unit test for the directive API input validation (empty text rejected, text over 500 chars rejected, valid text accepted)
acceptance_criteria: HumanDirective interface exists with id, text, createdAt, source, active fields
State projector handles 'human-directive' events and appends to humanDirectives array
State projector remains a pure function (no DB calls, no side effects)
Context assembler passes humanDirectives to the WorkspaceContext output
Prompt builder includes human directives in agent system prompts with clear delimitation
POST /api/sessions/[sessionId]/directives validates input and persists as event
API rejects empty strings and strings over 500 characters
Property-based tests verify deterministic replay, snapshot consistency, and isolation from unrelated events
verification: Since npm registry is blocked (INTEGRATIONS_ONLY), verify by reading all modified files and confirming: types compile logically, imports resolve to existing files, test patterns match existing test structure, switch/case has no gaps
findings: All implementations follow existing patterns. Added defensive null coalescing (?? []) for humanDirectives in context-assembler and prompt-builder to handle test mocks that use partial objects cast as 'any'. The Zod schema uses refine() for whitespace-only validation since Zod 4 min(1) only checks length, not content. Existing tests with 13-type event generators continue to work because the state projector default case handles unrecognized types gracefully.
